action.reasoning: Observation: I can see get_spherical_cameras source. It constructs c2w matrices and uses get_ray_directions/get_rays utilities. The render method passes rays_o[i], rays_d[i] per view. My current approach has camera convention issues. Analysis: I should use TripoSR's own camera construction code (from get_spherical_cameras) but with per-view azimuth/elevation/distance from cameras.json. Decision: Rewrite predict.py to use TripoSR's internal camera utilities directly, and handle background properly by outputting RGBA with white-to-transparent conversion.
